what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard research mandate that instructs agents to go to the live web and research topics that should inform a high-quality build: mobile/operator UI interaction patterns, Claude Code capabilities and MCP (especially the CHANNELS inject/notification path and its constraints), and other relevant external knowledge such as composition/registry-driven UI patterns.
observations: It is labeled as an external/online research mandate.
It targets interface design for mobile/operator UIs and durable interaction patterns.
It targets Claude Code capabilities, MCP, and specifically the CHANNELS mechanism described as the inject/notification path, with a version-sensitive research-preview caveat.
It points to an existing board web-research doc on MCP Channels + Claude Code and asks agents to verify and extend it.
It asks for diverse, contextually-grounded perspectives modeled on a prior multi-agent perspective sweep.
It explicitly states the goal is grounded understanding, not consensus or a borrowed plan.
It is filed as a current block by author session ch-3mpkjg3r in channel dragnet-development and links as part_of item-91983d4f.
standouts: The narrow focus on the CHANNELS inject/notification path and its constraints as a research target.
The instruction to verify and extend an existing board web-research doc rather than start from scratch.
The explicit framing that the goal is grounded understanding, not consensus or copying an external plan.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML frontmatter (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps) and a Markdown body.
Uses board:// URI addressing and a part_of link to another item.
Uses a title prefix F3 and an external/online qualifier.
concerns: The referenced web-research doc is not identified by address or id, so 'verify + extend' is under-specified.
The mandate is broad and lacks explicit deliverable format, success criteria, or timeline.
The 'research preview, version-sensitive' caveat means findings may become stale quickly and need versioning.
notes: A research dispatch note telling agents to check the live web for UI patterns and Claude Code/MCP CHANNELS details, while warning not to just copy a plan.
questions: What is the exact address or id of the board web-research doc on MCP Channels + Claude Code?
What specific prior multi-agent perspective sweep is being referenced as the model?
What does 'CHANNELS mechanism (the inject/notification path)' refer to in concrete API or behavior terms?
What is item-91983d4f and how does this mandate relate to it?
How will agents be dispatched and where will their outputs be recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a directive to dispatch external/online research to inform the build.
apparent_themes: Grounded external research over borrowed plans.
Durability and quality of operator/mobile interfaces.
intention_signals: It is trying to set up a focused, externally-oriented research brief that gathers diverse live-web perspectives to inform design and tooling choices, while guarding against simply adopting someone else's plan.
novelty: It explicitly rejects consensus and borrowed plans as goals, singles out the CHANNELS inject/notification path as a specific research object, and models the inquiry on a prior multi-agent perspective sweep.
contribution: This file contributes a scoped, externally-oriented research brief that records what needs to be verified and extended about Claude Code MCP Channels and durable UI patterns, preserving the intent to ground design in diverse live-web perspectives.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard research mandate dispatching agents to the live web to study mobile/operator UI patterns, Claude Code/MCP CHANNELS behavior, and registry-driven UI patterns, with instructions to verify and extend an existing board doc and avoid consensus-driven outputs.
intention_for_embedding: To direct external research that informs a high-quality build by grounding design and tooling decisions in diverse, current online perspectives rather than borrowed plans.
